assignments.title: Basic Arithmetic Operations Quiz
assignments.description: In this quiz, students will demonstrate their understanding of basic arithmetic operations, including addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. The quiz will assess their ability to perform calculations and solve problems using these operations.
requirements: Students must complete a series of multiple-choice questions that test their knowledge of basic arithmetic operations.
Students must solve a set of problems that require the application of arithmetic operations to find the solution.
The quiz will have a time limit of 30 minutes.
Students are expected to show their work and calculations for each problem.

assignments.title: Introduction to Mathematical Reasoning Written Assignment
assignments.description: In this written assignment, you will demonstrate your understanding of basic mathematical concepts and develop your skills in mathematical reasoning. You are expected to provide clear and concise solutions to a series of problems that cover fundamental arithmetic operations, algebraic expressions, and logical reasoning.
requirements: Read and review chapters 1-2 in the textbook 'Mathematics: A Very Short Introduction' by Timothy Gowers.
Complete the following problems and provide detailed solutions:
Solve 5 arithmetic expressions with fractions and decimals.
Simplify 3 algebraic expressions using basic laws of arithmetic.
Explain the reasoning behind your solutions to 2 logical reasoning questions.
Your submission should include all work and solutions, clearly labeled and organized.
The assignment should be submitted in a format that is easy to read and understand (e.g., typed or neatly handwritten).

assignments.title: Algebra Basics Checklist
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will review the fundamental concepts of algebra, including variables, expressions, and basic equations. The goal is to ensure students have a solid understanding of algebra basics, which will be crucial for more advanced mathematical concepts throughout the course.
requirements: Review the sections in the textbook that cover variables, expressions, and basic equations.
Complete a self-assessment checklist that covers the following topics: defining variables, simplifying expressions, solving basic linear equations, and understanding the order of operations.
Verify your understanding by checking your answers against the textbook examples and solutions.
Submit your completed checklist to demonstrate your mastery of algebra basics.

assignments.title: Solving Simple Equations Quiz
assignments.description: For this quiz, students will need to demonstrate their ability to solve simple equations. The quiz will consist of a set of equations that require basic algebraic manipulations to solve for the variable. Students should be able to apply basic algebraic properties and operations to isolate the variable and find its value.
requirements: Solve linear equations with one variable
Apply basic algebraic properties (e.g., addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) to isolate the variable
Simplify expressions and equations
Show all work and steps to solve the equations

assignments.title: Geometric Concepts Written Assignment
assignments.description: For this written assignment, students will demonstrate their understanding of basic geometric concepts, including points, lines, angles, and planes. Students should provide clear and concise explanations, using relevant mathematical terminology and notation. The assignment will assess students' ability to apply geometric concepts to solve problems and explain their reasoning.
requirements: Define and explain the concepts of points, lines, angles, and planes, including their notation and representation.
Provide examples of each concept, illustrating their properties and relationships.
Solve problems involving basic geometric concepts, such as finding angle measures, identifying types of angles, and determining properties of lines and planes.
Use proper mathematical terminology and notation throughout the assignment.
Show all work and calculations, and provide clear explanations for each step.

assignments.title: Points, Lines, and Angles Checklist
assignments.description: In this checklist, students will review and demonstrate their understanding of fundamental geometric concepts, including points, lines, and angles. Students should identify and describe the characteristics of each concept, including definitions, properties, and relationships.
requirements: Define and explain the concept of a point in geometry.
Describe the different types of lines (e.g., line segment, ray, line) and their properties.
Identify and explain the different types of angles (e.g., acute, obtuse, right, straight) and their properties.
Provide examples of real-world applications of points, lines, and angles.
Complete a self-assessment checklist to verify understanding of the concepts.

assignments.title: Calculus Introduction Quiz
assignments.description: In this quiz, students will demonstrate their understanding of the basic concepts of calculus, including limits, derivatives, and the fundamental theorems of calculus. The quiz will assess students' ability to apply mathematical reasoning and problem-solving skills to solve calculus-related problems.
requirements: Read chapters 1-3 of the textbook 'Mathematics: A Very Short Introduction' by Timothy Gowers to review the basic concepts of calculus.
Complete practice problems related to limits and derivatives to reinforce understanding of the concepts.
Take a 30-minute quiz consisting of 10 multiple-choice questions and 2 short-answer questions that cover the introduction to calculus, limits, and derivatives.
Submit the quiz within the given time frame, following the submission guidelines provided in the course.

assignments.title: Limits and Derivatives Written Assignment
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will demonstrate their understanding of limits and derivatives by completing a series of problems that require them to apply mathematical concepts to solve real-world problems. Students should use the concepts learned in class to evaluate limits, find derivatives, and interpret the results in the context of the given problems.
requirements: Complete all assigned problems on limits and derivatives from the textbook, specifically focusing on evaluating limits using algebraic manipulation and finding derivatives using the power rule and sum rule.
Show all work and provide clear explanations for each problem, including a brief description of the mathematical concepts used and the steps taken to solve the problem.
Use proper mathematical notation and formatting throughout the assignment.
Submit the assignment in the required format, ensuring that all pages are numbered and the work is organized in a logical and easy-to-follow manner.

assignments.title: Equations and Inequalities Checklist
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will complete a checklist to demonstrate their understanding of equations and inequalities. The checklist should cover key concepts such as solving linear equations, graphing linear inequalities, and understanding the properties of inequalities. Students are expected to review the relevant sections in the textbook and complete the checklist accurately.
requirements: Define and explain the concept of linear equations and inequalities.
Solve simple linear equations and inequalities.
Graph linear inequalities on a coordinate plane.
Identify and apply the properties of inequalities (e.g., addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division properties).
Provide examples of real-world applications of equations and inequalities.

assignments.title: Graphing and Functions Quiz
assignments.description: For this quiz, students will need to demonstrate their understanding of graphing and functions by accurately identifying and interpreting various types of graphs, including linear, quadratic, and other basic functions. Students will be presented with a series of questions that require them to analyze graphs, identify key features such as intercepts, maxima, and minima, and apply their knowledge of functions to solve problems.
requirements: Students must complete all questions within the allotted time.
Students must achieve a minimum score of 70% to pass.
Students should review the textbook chapters on graphing and functions, specifically sections on linear and quadratic equations, before attempting the quiz.
Students should be able to identify and explain the key features of different types of graphs.

assignments.title: Geometric Measurements Written Assignment
assignments.description: For this written assignment, students will apply geometric measurement concepts to solve problems involving perimeter, area, and volume of various shapes. Students should refer to Chapter 5 of the textbook 'Mathematics: A Very Short Introduction' by Timothy Gowers, which covers geometric concepts. The assignment should include at least 5 problems that demonstrate an understanding of how to calculate perimeter, area, and volume of different shapes, including rectangles, triangles, circles, and 3D objects. Each problem should be clearly labeled and include any relevant diagrams or formulas used to solve the problem. The assignment should be typed and submitted as a single document.
requirements: Calculate the perimeter and area of at least 2 different polygons
Calculate the volume of at least 2 different 3D shapes
Include diagrams or illustrations to support calculations where necessary
Use proper mathematical notation and formulas throughout the assignment
Submit the assignment as a typed document with clear labels and headings

assignments.title: Perimeter, Area, and Volume Checklist
assignments.description: For this assignment, students will complete a checklist to demonstrate their understanding of perimeter, area, and volume concepts. The checklist will cover various geometric shapes, including triangles, quadrilaterals, polygons, and 3D objects. Students should review the relevant sections in the textbook, 'Mathematics: A Very Short Introduction' by Timothy Gowers, and ensure they can identify and calculate perimeter, area, and volume for each shape.
requirements: Define and explain the concepts of perimeter, area, and volume.
Identify and calculate the perimeter of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons.
Identify and calculate the area of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons.
Identify and calculate the volume of 3D objects, including prisms and cylinders.
Complete a checklist with specific problems and examples to demonstrate understanding.

assignments.title: Mathematical Modeling Quiz
assignments.description: For this quiz, students will apply mathematical concepts to real-world problems. They will analyze a given scenario, identify the relevant mathematical models, and use problem-solving skills to select the correct answers. The quiz will assess students' ability to think critically and make connections between mathematical principles and practical applications.
requirements: Read and understand the quiz questions and any provided context.
Identify the relevant mathematical concepts and models applicable to each question.
Apply problem-solving strategies to arrive at the correct answers.
Select the correct answers from the provided options.

assignments.title: Real-World Applications Written Assignment
assignments.description: For this written assignment, you will apply mathematical concepts to real-world scenarios. You are expected to select a real-world problem or scenario that interests you and describe how mathematical principles can be used to analyze or solve it. Your response should clearly articulate the problem, explain the mathematical concepts relevant to the problem, and demonstrate how these concepts are applied to find a solution or interpret the results.
requirements: The assignment should be 2-3 pages in length, not including any references or appendices.
Include a clear and concise statement of the problem.
Identify and explain the relevant mathematical concepts and principles.
Apply the mathematical concepts to analyze or solve the problem.
Provide examples or case studies to support your analysis.
Cite any sources used in your analysis.
Submit your assignment in a format compatible with common word processing software.

assignments.title: Key Concepts Review Checklist
assignments.description: In this assignment, you will review and verify your understanding of the key concepts covered throughout the course. You will go through a series of statements or criteria that outline the main ideas and skills you have learned, and you will confirm that you can recall and apply them accurately.
requirements: Review the course materials and notes from weeks 1 to 7.
Go through the checklist of key concepts provided.
For each item on the checklist, verify that you understand the concept and can provide a brief example or explanation.
Submit your completed checklist with confirmation of your understanding for each key concept.

assignments.title: Practice Problems and Review Quiz
assignments.description: For this quiz, students will review and practice problems covering key concepts from the entire course. The quiz will assess students' understanding of mathematical principles and concepts, including algebra, geometry, calculus, and mathematical reasoning. Students should expect to solve problems that require application of mathematical concepts to various scenarios.
requirements: Complete a set of multiple-choice questions that test understanding of algebraic operations, geometric concepts, and calculus principles.
Solve short-answer problems that require mathematical reasoning and application of concepts learned throughout the course.
Review and practice problems from the textbook, specifically chapters related to algebra, geometry, and calculus.
